title: Oritrophium nevadense
taxonomy: Oritrophium nevadense is classified within the plant family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family encompasses a vast array of flowering plants, with over 23,000 known species worldwide. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Oritrophium nevadense is likely to be closely related to other flowering plants that share similar characteristics, such as composite flowers. The classification of Oritrophium nevadense is also subject to change as new research emerges and our understanding of plant relationships evolves. Further taxonomic analysis may be necessary to confirm the specific classification of this plant.

preparations: Oritrophium nevadense, also known as the Nevada orris root, is a plant native to the southwestern United States. intro: Oritrophium nevadense, also known as Nevada copperflower, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate conditions such as pain, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Oritrophium nevadense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nevada copperflower include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
